title: OSI Model Layers
content:
Layer 7 Application — HTTP, FTP, DNS
Layer 6 Presentation — Encryption, compression
Layer 5 Session — Sessions between apps
Layer 4 Transport — TCP/UDP, ports
Layer 3 Network — IP addressing, routing
Layer 2 Data Link — MAC addresses, switches
Layer 1 Physical — Cables, hubs, signals

title: Common Attack Vectors
content:
Phishing — social engineering via email
MITM — intercept traffic between two parties
DDoS — overwhelm a target with traffic
SQL Injection — malicious SQL in input fields
XSS — inject scripts into web pages
Buffer Overflow — exceed memory buffer limits

title: Nmap Cheat Sheet
content:
nmap -sV target — version detection
nmap -O target — OS detection
nmap -A target — aggressive scan
nmap -p 1-65535 target — all ports
nmap --script vuln target — vulnerability scan

title: Metasploit Workflow
content:
1. msfconsole — start Metasploit
2. search <exploit> — find module
3. use <module> — select module
4. show options — view required params
5. set RHOSTS <target> — set target
6. set PAYLOAD — choose payload
7. exploit — launch attack

title: Security+ Domain Weights
content:
Threats, Attacks & Vulnerabilities — 24%
Architecture & Design — 21%
Implementation — 25%
Operations & Incident Response — 16%
Governance, Risk & Compliance — 14%

title: Cryptography Basics
content:
Symmetric — same key for encrypt/decrypt (AES, DES)
Asymmetric — public/private key pair (RSA, ECC)
Hashing — one-way function (SHA-256, MD5)
PKI — infrastructure for managing digital certificates
SSL/TLS — secure communication over networks

title: test
content:
# OSI Model — Network Security Notes

## Overview
The OSI model has **7 layers** that describe how data travels 
across a network. Understanding each layer is *critical* for 
identifying attack surfaces.

## The 7 Layers

1. Physical — cables, switches, hardware
2. Data Link — MAC addresses, frames
3. Network — IP addresses, routing
4. Transport — TCP/UDP, ports
5. Session — connections, authentication
6. Presentation — encryption, compression
7. Application — HTTP, DNS, FTP

## Common Attacks by Layer

- **Layer 2:** ARP Spoofing, MAC flooding
- **Layer 3:** IP Spoofing, ICMP attacks
- **Layer 4:** SYN flood, port scanning
- **Layer 7:** SQL injection, XSS, phishing

## Key Command
```bash
nmap -sV -p 1-1000 192.168.1.1
```

> Remember: Security is only as strong as the weakest layer.

---

## Study Checklist
- [ ] Memorize all 7 layers
- [ ] Understand TCP vs UDP
- [ ] Practice with Wireshark
